steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The goal is to make a generalization about minerals. A generalization states a principle that applies broadly to a category, not just a description of the specific minerals (calcite 3, apatite 5, topaz 8) named in the notes. The notes define the rule: minerals with larger Mohs numbers can scratch minerals with smaller numbers. The right answer must turn that rule into a broadly applicable statement.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices, confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Based on their Mohs scale numbers, topaz (8) is harder than apatite (5), and apatite is harder than calcite (3).': This simply ranks the three specific minerals from the notes — it is a list of particular facts, not a generalization. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Topaz can leave visible scratches on calcite, which is why topaz has a higher number than calcite on the Mohs scale of mineral hardness.': This describes a relationship between two specific named minerals; it does not generalize to all minerals. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Any mineral with a Mohs number of 8, like topaz, can scratch a mineral with a Mohs number of 5, like apatite.': The word any makes this a principle that applies to all minerals with that number, not just topaz. Topaz and apatite are used as examples, not as the entire claim. ✓
  • Choice D — 'The Mohs scale can be used to order calcite, topaz, and apatite by their ability to scratch other minerals.': This describes what the scale can do with the three listed minerals, but it does not make a broader claim about minerals in general. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
